name: Cactus Jelly
recipeIngredient: 1 quart fruit (e.g., prickly pear cactus pads)
recipeInstructions:
1. Gather about 1 quart of fruit to make 2-1/2 cups juice. If most of the fruit is very ripe, try to include some under-ripe ones to add pectin.
2. Wash the fruit and place it in a kettle with just enough water to cover.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil until quite tender and soft (extracts pectin and flavor).
4. Strain through two thicknesses of cheesecloth and set juice aside so sediment will settle. For clear jelly, do not use the sediment portion.
5. For each 2-1/2 cups juice, add one package of powdered pectin (more successful than liquid). Bring to a fast boil, stirring constantly.
6. Add 3 tablespoons lemon or lime juice and 3-1/2 cups sugar. Bring to a hard boil (one you can't press down with a spoon) and boil for three minutes. The timing is very important to get a product that will jell.
7. Remove from heat, skim, and pour into sterilized jelly glasses. Cover with a layer of melted paraffin.
(Note: I am experimenting with a non-sugar version using apple, pear, and white grape juice concentrate but currently have it all in freezer waiting for some time to try different quantities, etc.)
